description: Major unsolved conjectures in number theory - primes, Diophantine equations, and arithmetic.

facts.truth: every even integer greater than 2 is the sum of two primes
distractors: every odd integer is the sum of two primes
every integer is prime or the sum of two primes

facts.context: The weak Goldbach conjecture (odd Goldbach) states every odd n>5 is sum of three primes. Its status is:
The Twin Prime Conjecture states that:
facts.truth: there are infinitely many pairs of primes differing by 2
distractors: twin primes are finite in number
all primes greater than 3 are twins

facts.context: Zhang's 2013 breakthrough on bounded gaps between primes showed:
facts.truth: infinitely many prime pairs with gap at most 70 million (now reduced to 246)
distractors: the twin prime conjecture is true
there are only finitely many close primes

facts.context: The Collatz Conjecture states that iterating n→n/2 (even) or n→3n+1 (odd):
facts.truth: eventually reaches 1 for all positive integers
distractors: has been proven for all integers

facts.context: Computational verification of the Collatz Conjecture has confirmed it for:
The ABC Conjecture relates the prime factors of a, b, and a+b=c. It states:
facts.truth: for any ε>0, there are finitely many triples where c > rad(abc)^(1+ε)
distractors: the radical is always larger than c
the conjecture has been definitively proven

facts.context: Mochizuki's claimed proof of ABC using Inter-universal Teichmüller Theory (IUT):
facts.truth: remains controversial with key steps disputed by Scholze and Stix
distractors: does not address ABC at all

facts.context: Legendre's Conjecture states that between n² and (n+1)² there is:
A Sophie Germain prime p satisfies that 2p+1 is also prime. The conjecture states:
facts.truth: there are infinitely many Sophie Germain primes (unproven)

facts.context: Fermat numbers F_n = 2^(2^n) + 1 are prime for n=0,1,2,3,4. The conjecture on Fermat primes:
facts.truth: whether infinitely many exist is unknown; none found for n>4

facts.context: Mersenne primes have form 2^p - 1 where p is prime. It is conjectured that:
facts.truth: infinitely many Mersenne primes exist (unproven, but 51 known)
distractors: all 2^p - 1 are prime when p is prime
the largest possible has been found
